questions.question:
The table below shows the number of coffee orders at a cafe.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|}\hline \text{Size} & \text{Number of Orders} \\ \hline \text{Small} & 12 \\ \hline \text{Medium} & 25 \\ \hline \text{Large} & 13 \\ \hline\end{array}$$

If a pie chart were created to represent this data, what would be the measure of the central angle for the 'Medium' category?
questions.explanation: The total number of orders is $12 + 25 + 13 = 50$. The proportion of Medium orders is $25/50 = 0.5$. To find the central angle, multiply the proportion by the total degrees in a circle: $0.5 \times 360 = 180$.

questions.question:
The line graph below tracks the price of a stock over 5 days.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|}\hline \text{Day} & \text{Stock Price} \\ \hline 1 & \$10 \\ \hline 2 & \$12 \\ \hline 3 & \$11 \\ \hline 4 & \$15 \\ \hline 5 & \$14 \\ \hline\end{array}$$

Between which two consecutive days did the stock price show the greatest percent increase?
questions.explanation: From Day 1 to 2, the increase is $2/10 = 20\%$. From Day 3 to 4, the increase is $4/11 \approx 36.4\%$. Day 2 to 3 and Day 4 to 5 represent decreases.

questions.question: In a scatterplot showing the relationship between hours studied ($x$) and exam score ($y$), the line of best fit is $y = 7x + 45$. Based on this model, what is the predicted score for a student who studied for 6 hours?

The frequency table below shows the number of books read by students in a month.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|}\hline \text{Number of Books} & \text{Number of Students} \\ \hline 0 & 2 \\ \hline 1 & 5 \\ \hline 2 & 8 \\ \hline 3 & 5 \\ \hline\end{array}$$

What is the median number of books read?
questions.explanation: The total number of students is $2 + 5 + 8 + 5 = 20$. The median is the average of the 10th and 11th values. Counting through the frequencies: the first 2 are '0', the next 5 (up to 7th) are '1', and the next 8 (up to 15th) are '2'. Both the 10th and 11th values are '2'.

questions.question:
The bar chart below compares the rainfall in four cities.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|}\hline \text{City} & \text{Rainfall (inches)} \\ \hline \text{A} & 4 \\ \hline \text{B} & 6 \\ \hline \text{C} & 2 \\ \hline \text{D} & 8 \\ \hline\end{array}$$

What is the mean rainfall, in inches, for these four cities?
questions.explanation: To find the mean, sum the rainfall amounts and divide by the number of cities: $(4 + 6 + 2 + 8) / 4 = 20 / 4 = 5$.

questions.question: A researcher uses a scatterplot to model the relationship between the age of a tree, $a$, in years, and its height, $h$, in meters. The line of best fit is $h = 0.5a + 2$. Which of the following is the best interpretation of the number 2 in this context?
options: The predicted increase in height each year
The predicted height of the tree when it is 0 years old
The maximum height the tree can reach
The number of years it takes for the tree to grow 1 meter
questions.explanation: In the linear model $h = ma + b$, the constant $b$ (which is 2) represents the $y$-intercept. This is the value of $h$ when the age $a$ is 0.

questions.question:
The table below shows the distribution of votes for four candidates in a local election. If 4,000 people voted in total, how many more votes did Candidate A receive than Candidate C?

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|}\hline \text{Candidate} & \text{Percent of Votes} \\ \hline \text{A} & 35\% \\ \hline \text{B} & 30\% \\ \hline \text{C} & 20\% \\ \hline \text{D} & 15\% \\ \hline\end{array}$$
questions.explanation: The difference in percentages between Candidate A and Candidate C is $35\% - 20\% = 15\%$. To find the number of votes, calculate $15\%$ of 4,000: $0.15 \times 4{,}000 = 600$.

questions.question: A histogram shows the number of hours 25 students spent volunteering last month. The '10–14 hours' interval has a frequency of 6. What percent of the students spent between 10 and 14 hours volunteering?
questions.explanation: The percentage is calculated by dividing the frequency of the interval by the total number of students: $(6 / 25) \times 100 = 0.24 \times 100 = 24\%$.

questions.question: A box plot represents the test scores of a class. The median score is 82, the first quartile is 75, and the third quartile is 90. Which of the following statements must be true?
options: The lowest score in the class is 75.
Exactly 50% of the scores are between 75 and 90.
The range of the scores is 15.
questions.explanation: By definition, the interquartile range (from $Q1$ to $Q3$) contains the middle 50% of the data. Since $Q1 = 75$ and $Q3 = 90$, 50% of the scores fall in this range.

questions.question: The graph of a quadratic function $f(x)$ shows a vertex at $(3, -4)$ and $x$-intercepts at $(1, 0)$ and $(5, 0)$. Which of the following is the minimum value of the function?
questions.explanation: For a parabola that opens upward (implied by the vertex being below the $x$-intercepts), the minimum value of the function is the $y$-coordinate of the vertex. Here, that value is $-4$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent work! You can confidently interpret data from tables, graphs, and charts, calculate central angles, find medians from frequency distributions, and apply lines of best fit.
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review missed questions and focus on carefully reading graph axes and labels, and remember that the median position depends on the total count, not just the frequencies.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Remember: the $y$-intercept is the value when $x=0$, the interquartile range always contains the middle 50% of data, and percent change requires dividing by the original value.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! Start by identifying what each graph type shows (pie = parts of whole, line = trends over time, scatter = relationships), then practice extracting specific values and applying basic formulas.
